claim: The YOLO harness boundary audit in src/demand/evidence-dispatch.ts implements buildBoundarySnapshot and diffBoundarySnapshots to detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in both git-backed and non-git projects, with test coverage for gitignored files and artifact exclusion.

evidence.summary: gitFiles function spawns git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z to get tracked, cached, and other files
evidence.why: Git-backed detection uses native git to enumerate all tracked and untracked files except those in exclude-standard

evidence.summary: walkFiles function recursively walks filesystem and excludes .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: Fallback walk provides coverage for non-git projects and captures files git ls-files might miss

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot combines git and walk files then computes SHA-256 digests, excluding allowedRoots
evidence.why: Combined strategy ensures git-backed projects use both sources while non-git projects fall back to walk only

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after Maps to detect added, modified, and deleted files
evidence.why: Diff logic detects any mutation in the project file set between boundary snapshots

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundaryBefore snapshot before agent execution with output_dir in allowedRoots
evidence.why: Pre-execution snapshot establishes baseline with artifact root excluded from mutation detection

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundaryAfter snapshot, diffs changes, and sets project_mutation violated when changes exist
evidence.why: Post-execution diff detects violations and blocks PRD readiness when mutations are found

evidence.summary: Test git-backed boundary detects untracked-mutation.txt added by agent
evidence.why: Verifies git ls-files catches untracked files outside artifact root

evidence.summary: Test git-backed boundary detects .env mutation even with .env present in .gitignore
evidence.why: Verifies detection of gitignored files that are still outside excluded directories

evidence.summary: Test non-git boundary fallback detects modified src/existing.txt and ignores agent-scratch.txt artifact
evidence.why: Verifies walkFiles fallback works and correctly excludes allowed artifact root from detection

evidence.summary: excludedDir function excludes .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache from walk
evidence.why: Prevents false positive mutations in standard build and dependency directories

evidence.summary: Union of git files and walk files with Set deduplication ensures complete coverage
evidence.why: Combines sources in git-backed projects to catch files git might not report in some edge cases

evidence.summary: Test boundary blocks project mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: Verifies boundary enforcement is independent of agent evidence quality

assumptions: Claude -p agents with full tools could still create files outside the artifact root; boundary audit would detect this as a violation
Stress testing with real Claude agents is required to verify boundary enforcement holds against sophisticated mutation attempts
Git ls-files -co covers tracked, cached, and other untracked files but may have edge cases with complex gitignore rules
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without triggering readiness blockers if boundary snapshot logic has gaps
Complex gitignore patterns or sparse checkouts might cause git ls-files to miss some files that walkFiles would catch
Filesystem walk performance may degrade on very large project trees with deep nesting

result.notes: Boundary snapshot implementation is present and tested. Git-backed detection combines git ls-files with walk fallback. Non-git projects use walk only. Tests cover untracked files, gitignored files, and artifact exclusion. Stress testing with real Claude agents remains to validate behavior in production scenarios.
